buggy_code:
X = prepare_features(df)
y = prepare_targets(df)

model = RandomForestRegressor()
model.fit(X, y)

# Evaluate on SAME data used for training
preds = model.predict(X)
performance = mean_squared_error(y, preds)
print(f'Performance: {performance}')
clean_code:
X = prepare_features(df)
y = prepare_targets(df)

# Split into train and test
X_train, X_test = X[:800], X[800:]
y_train, y_test = y[:800], y[800:]

model = RandomForestRegressor()
model.fit(X_train, y_train)

# Evaluate on DIFFERENT data (out-of-sample)
preds = model.predict(X_test)
performance = mean_squared_error(y_test, preds)
print(f'Performance (out-of-sample): {performance}')
description: Model is trained and evaluated on the same data.
explanation: When you fit a model on data and then evaluate it on the same data, the performance metrics (accuracy, R², MSE, etc.) are biased upward because the model has already seen those examples. This leads to overestimated performance. In a backtest, this would show fake profits because the model memorized the training data rather than learning generalizable patterns.
suggested_fix: Always evaluate on a held-out test set that was NOT used during training. Use train/test split or cross-validation.

issues.title: Model evaluated on training data only
